reasoning: Relapsing fever is a bacterial infection (caused by Borrelia species) that is readily treated with antibiotics (like doxycycline). There is no existing human vaccine for this disease, nor is there any significant, late-stage vaccine candidate in clinical trials (even Lyme disease, a much more prominent Borrelia infection, does not currently have a fully approved human vaccine, though one is in Phase 3). Vaccine development typically takes many years. It is virtually impossible for a new vaccine against relapsing fever to be developed, approved, and added to the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' within the 1- to 3-month forecast horizon. I assign the minimum allowable probability (0.05) to the most immediate date, increasing slightly for later dates to reflect marginal time-horizon uncertainty.
